caption: Figure 1: Projected cumulative incidence of drift types by interaction count in simulation frame- work. Semantic drift emerges earliest and affects nearly half of agents by 600 interactions, while behavioral drift shows slower but steady progression. Data aggregated across 847 simulated workflows.
description2: The figure plots the projected cumulative incidence of drift types by interaction count in a simulation framework, showing semantic and behavioral drift emergence.
description3: Two-panel line chart displays projected cumulative incidence of drift types, showing semantic, coordination, and behavioral drift over cumulative agent interactions.
questions: How do different types of drift emerge and progress over the course of agent interactions?
What is the relative rate of progression for different types of drift within the simulation?

caption: Figure 2: Degradation of ASI component categories over extended interactions. Behavioral boundaries show steepest decline, indicating progressive emergence of unintended strategies. All components converge toward critical thresholds by 500 interactions.
description2: The figure plots the degradation of ASI component categories over extended interactions, with behavioral boundaries declining steeply and all components converging towards critical thresholds.
description3: Two-panel line chart displays normalized component scores over interaction count, showing four categories converging toward critical thresholds.
questions: How do different components change over time during extended interactions?
What is the general trend of component performance as interactions progress?

caption: Figure 3: Drift susceptibility by architectural characteristics at 300 interactions. Two-level hierarchies and explicit memory systems show greatest stability. Error bars represent 95% confidence intervals.
description2: The figure plots drift susceptibility by architectural characteristics, with two-level hierarchies and explicit memory systems exhibiting the greatest stability, accompanied by error bars representing 95% confidence intervals.
description3: A bar chart displays median ASI at 300 interactions, with error bars, across several architectural characteristics.
questions: What architectural features influence the observed susceptibility to drift?
How do confidence intervals illustrate the variability in system characteristics?
